title: Stale comment on LocalKey::RsaPss2048 — says no per-call clone but spawn_blocking clones
description: The LocalKey enum comment at the RsaPss2048 variant says "Store the ready-to-sign key... so that sign() can use &self without cloning." But the spawn_blocking implementation added in round 5 does a per-call clone (key_clone = signing_key.clone()) to satisfy the 'static bound. The original motivation (avoid per-call copy of key material) is no longer true. The comment should be updated to explain: (a) the clone is required for spawn_blocking, (b) rsa::pss::SigningKey<Sha256> implements ZeroizeOnDrop so the clone is zeroized on drop — or if it does not, flag that as the real issue.
